data.narrative:
Astroscale, a public Space & Aerospace company, is valued at $900M against a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), and Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B).

A peer-median methodology is applicable and yields a $7.0B implied valuation. This reflects the median across the three-company cohort, with a range spanning $6.3B to $37.0B. The peer-anchoring approach, while not deployable here due to the absence of disclosed revenue, would reference a 45.3x P/S median across 6 public Space & Aerospace tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL). The wide peer dispersion—from Firefly at $6.3B to Rocket Lab at $37.0B—underscores significant valuation variance within the sector.

Triangulation across available methods supports a valuation range of $6.0B to $8.0B, centered at a $7.0B median. The current $900M valuation sits substantially below this derived range, implying either material execution or market-adoption risk priced in, or a potential valuation gap. Key uncertainties include revenue disclosure (blocking P/S application), stage-specific capital structure differences across the peer set, and the outsized influence of Rocket Lab's $37.0B valuation on sector benchmarking. A second comparable method would materially strengthen conviction in any derived range.
